benchmark_scope_note: OPT-04 qualitative benchmark for a mockup-first workflow only. It does not authorize product UI implementation, source changes, GitHub write, live Bridge, Dispatcher, Runner, Codex runtime task execution, dependency installation, RV2-04, or OPT-05.

benchmark_cases.name: Pure prose UI instruction
manual_ui_prompt_observations: reviewer must infer screen hierarchy from prose
acceptance criteria can mix layout, behavior, and authority assumptions
mockup_first_observations: layout and information hierarchy are visible before implementation
component list and non-goals are explicit in the UI contract
review can separate visual intent from implementation authority
semantic approval remains outside the mockup

benchmark_cases.name: Review Packet / Evidence Summary concept
manual_ui_prompt_observations: review evidence screens can become vague without concrete regions
safety assertions may be confused with task acceptance
raw artifacts can be under-emphasized in prose-only descriptions
mockup_first_observations: validation summary, command results, changed artifacts, and safety assertions are distinct
the mockup labels semantic review as pending
artifact references are visible as first-class review items
the mockup remains static and disconnected from app runtime

benchmark_cases.name: Future implementation task shaping
manual_ui_prompt_observations: future Codex packets may need extra wording to avoid source and authority expansion
reviewers may ask for clarification about what the UI is allowed to decide
accessibility and state requirements can be easy to omit
mockup_first_observations: the UI contract names states, accessibility notes, acceptance checks, and non-goals
the mockup can reduce vague instructions while staying subordinate to current app truth
a later implementation task still requires fresh source review and separate approval

manual_ui_prompt_observations.summary: Pure prose UI instructions can work for small changes but tend to blur layout intent, component boundaries, states, and review acceptance criteria.
main_costs: risk that safety assertions and semantic approval are conflated

mockup_first_observations.summary: A static mockup plus UI contract makes review intent more concrete while preserving separate source review and authority checks.
limitations: mockup does not represent current app truth
mockup does not decide implementation architecture
mockup does not replace accessibility or source review
qualitative benchmark does not provide precise timing or percentage metrics

conditions: one small next planning step only
mockup remains subordinate to current app truth
no product UI implementation from OPT-04
no_go_conditions: mockup treated as sole source of truth
source, tests, scripts, or config changes are needed
metrics are converted into unsupported precise percentages
